steps.title: Calculate the Third Side Length
steps.content:
The perimeter of a triangle is given by the sum of its side lengths. Since the lengths of two sides are known to be 5 cm and 7 cm, and the total perimeter is 16 cm, we can find the third side length.

Let \( x \) represent the length of the third side. The equation for the perimeter then becomes:$$5 + 7 + x = 16$$

Solve this equation to find \( x \):

steps.title: Determine the Correct Value for X
steps.content:
$$\begin{align*} 5 + 7 + x &= 16 \\ x &= 16 - (5 + 7) \\ x &= 4\end{align*}$$
  • The calculated value of \(x\) matches one of the options provided in the list.
